column1: It's not polite to steal from your 'airy little 'ost.  You seem to have made the 'ermit un'appy.
column2: 從你那髮毛叢生的小房東那兒偷東西實在不禮貌。看來你惹得這隱士很不開心。

column1: A single candle sits upon the table.
column2: 一根蠟燭孤獨地擺在桌上。

column1: You'll have to get up first.
column2: 你得先起身才行。

column1: That's the swinging, rocking cave entrance.
column2: 那是搖晃擺動的洞穴入口。

column1: It's a chair carved out of rough stone.
column2: 這是用粗糙石頭雕刻出來的椅子。

column1: The fire is down to a set of dimly glowing coals.
column2: 火焰只剩下微微發光的炭火。

column1: You see a cot.
column2: 你看到一張簡易床。

column1: The fire has mostly burned down.  It doesn't seem to be accomplishing much in the way of heat or light for the cave.
column2: 火焰已燒得差不多了。對洞穴的溫度和照明幫助似乎不大。

column1: No thanks.  I'm not much o' one for those tree snacks.
column2: 「不了。我不太喜歡那些樹上的零食。」

column1: Nasty sorts, they is.  I sees them sneaking arund the woods all the times. Uther then the Warlock, I 'as nothing to do with them.
column2: 「那些傢伙很討厭。我常常看到它們在樹林裡偷偷摸摸。除了巫術師，我和它們沒什麼關係。」

column1: Well, it's dark and damp and the waters roars sos there's not a moment's quiet.  The ceiling drips and the walls are slimy and it's always cold.
column2: 「嗯，這地方又黑又潮濕，水聲咆哮得沒一刻安寧。天花板滴水，牆壁黏糊糊的，永遠冷得要命。」

column1: But, oh well, sez I, it's 'ome.
column2: 「但，嗯，我說啊，這是家。」

column1: I likes to play the game.  I can show you how to play, if you likes.
column2: 「我喜歡玩遊戲。要是你有興趣，我可以教你怎麼玩。」

column1: E's me friend, 'e and Fenrus.  Powerful wizard 'e is.
column2: 「他是我的朋友，他和芬魯斯。他是位強大的巫師。」

column1: I 'ere 'is 'ouse is sumthin' to see up north.
column2: 「我聽說他的房子在北邊，值得一看。」

column1: I don't make it out much, and 'e's pretty busy, sos I don't see 'em too often.  But when we do all get together, you can be sure we'll 'ave fun.
column2: 「我出門不多，他也很忙，所以我不常見到他們。但當我們聚在一起時，保證會很開心。」

column1: 'E loves to play 'is games, 'e do.
column2: 「他喜歡玩他的遊戲，真的。」

column1: This 'ere's wot's called the Flying Falls.
column2: 「這個地方叫做飛瀑。」

column1: I've doon sum flying falls arund 'ere meself.  That porch gets slippery sumtimes.  He, heh, he.
column2: 「我自己也在這附近飛過瀑布。那個陽台有時會很滑。哈，呵呵，呵。」

column1: Me family lives pretty far apart from each other.  We're all 'ermits, you know.
column2: 「我們一家住得很散。我們都是隱士。」

column1: Fenrus is Erasmus' familiar or is that the other way around?
column2: 「芬魯斯是伊拉斯謨的助手，還是說反過來啊？」

column1: Erasmus' favorite game is the Mage's Maze.  'Ave to be a magic user to play it, though.  Erasmus is awful good at it.
column2: 「伊拉斯謨最喜歡的遊戲是法師迷宮。雖然只有法師才能玩。伊拉斯謨特別擅長。」

column1: An 'ermit is one wot lives alone, far away from them wot talks too much or asks too many questions.  'Ermits are shy, quiet types wot don't say much.
column2: 「隱士就是獨自生活，遠離那些話太多或問題太多的傢伙。隱士是害羞、安靜、話不多的人。」

column1: Me brother 'Arry 'ardly says a word in a year.  Me sister 'Ortense 'asn't spoken since she were six.  I never talks at all.  Yes, we 'ermits nose how to keep a mouth shut.
column2: 「我哥哥哈瑞一年幾乎說不了一句話。我妹妹霍頓絲從六歲起就沒說過話。我根本不開口。是啊，我們隱士知道怎麼守口如瓶。」

column1: I nose sum magic.  Erasmus the wizard tot me sum spells.
column2: 「我懂一些魔法。法師伊拉斯謨教了我一些法術。」

column1: He's wot put the spell on me ladder sos the brigands don't get up.  Them wot climbs th' rocks gets a door opening in their nose.
column2: 「他就是在我的梯子上施法，讓山賊爬不上去。那些爬岩石的傢伙會被門打在鼻子上。」

column1: A magic mirror of reflection, it was.  If you use it, when a nasty spell was cast at you, it was wot sent it back at the one wot cast it.  Do un to others, I sez.  Heh, he, he.
column2: 「那是一面魔法反射鏡。要是有人對你施壞法術，它會把法術反射回施術者。就是以其人之身還治其人之身。哈，呵呵，呵。」

column1: I'm 'Enry.  'Enry the eighth I is.  Me farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry...
column2: 「我是亨利。我是第八個亨利。我爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利…」

column1: ... and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry.  And every one was an 'ermit.
column2: 「…他爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利。每一個都是隱士。」

column1: Yes, I still have me magic scroll, but you didn't seem to want it. 'Ave you changed your mind?
column2: 「是啊，我還有我的魔法捲軸，但你似乎不想要。你改主意了嗎？」

column1: You already 'ave me only scroll.
column2: 「你已經有我唯一的捲軸了。」

column1: If you be wanting to learn the spell, I'll be pleased to share it to you. It's just getting molding in me 'iding place.  Do you want it?
column2: 「要是你想學法術，我樂意和你分享。它在我的躲藏地方發霉呢。你要嗎？」

column1: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there.  You're welcome to stay if you share some rations and play some cribbage.
column2: 「我的客用床是那邊角落的稻草。要是你願意分享口糧和我玩幾盤牌戲，歡迎你留下。」

column1: The spells wot I nose is pretty simple maybe, but I like 'em.
column2: 「我懂的法術或許很簡單，但我喜歡。」

column1: I can make me ladder show up and me door close and even get rid o' pests using the trigger spell, you see.
column2: 「我可以用觸發術讓梯子出現，讓門關上，甚至除掉害蟲，你看。」

column1: It's the spell wot sets off uther spells.
column2: 「那是用來啟動其他法術的法術。」

column1: Like when you wants to see the ladder, you casts a trigger and there you 'ave it, large as life.
column2: 「比如說，你想看梯子時，你就施一個觸發術，嘿，梯子就在眼前。」

column1: Course, you gots to 'ave a spell on the invisible ladder to make it visible first.  But it 'as its uses.
column2: 「當然，你得先對隱形的梯子施法讓它現身。但它確實有用。」

column1: I thinks I 'ave a scroll with the spell, if you be knowing sum magic, that is.
column2: 「我想我有個捲軸裡有法術，假如你懂點魔法的話。」

column1: E's not so bad.  Got a good sense o' 'umor, 'e 'as.  I get the giggles just to think of 'im.  He heh he heh he.
column2: 「他還不錯。很有幽默感。我一想到他就咯咯笑。哈哈哈哈哈。」

column1: 'E's come by at times to chat.  Borrowed the mirror wot I borrowed from Erasmus 'e did.
column2: 「他有時會來聊天。他借走了我從伊拉斯謨那兒借來的鏡子。」

column1: You seem to have made the 'ermit un'appy.
column2: 看來你惹得這隱士很不開心。

column1: You see the shaggiest little man (you think it's a man, anyway) you've ever seen.
column2: 你看到一個毛髮蓬亂的小個子（你想應該是個男人啦）。

column1: 'Enry is wide awake, and that makes it impossible for you to sleep.
column2: 亨利毫無睡意，這讓你根本睡不著。

column1: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there.  You're welcome to stay the night since you've shared your rations.  When you want to sleep, just say so.
column2: 「我的客用床是那邊角落的稻草。既然你分享了口糧，歡迎你留下睡一晚。想睡的時候就說一聲。」

column1: You can't share what you don't have.
column2: 你沒有的東西無法分享。

column1: You can't sleep in this old rickety chair.  Get up and sleep on the bed of straw.
column2: 你沒法在這把老舊搖搖晃晃的椅子上睡覺。起來睡稻草床吧。

column1: Cave
column2: 洞穴

column1: Cribbage
column2: 牌戲

column1: Falls
column2: 瀑布

column1: Family
column2: 家族

column1: Games
column2: 遊戲

column1: Ladder
column2: 梯子

column1: Mirror
column2: 鏡子

column1: Scroll
column2: 捲軸

column1: Sleep
column2: 睡眠

column1: It's a round chair.
column2: 這是張圓椅子。

column1: It's a relic from India, a snake charmer's basket.
column2: 這是來自印度的古物，印度吹蛇者的籃子。

column1: Every 'ermit ought to 'ave a few urns around, sez I.
column2: 「每個隱士都應該放些罈子在周圍，我是這麼說的。」

column1: It's an ancient Egyptian pot from the tomb of Anon-Ha.
column2: 這是來自古埃及安農—哈墓穴的陶罐。

column1: A place to keep grain for 'ermit's ale.
column2: 用來存放隱士啤酒穀物的地方。

column1: 'Enry always keeps a good supply of coal on hand.
column2: 亨利總是備著充足的煤炭。

column1: What's left of 'Enry's dinner.
column2: 亨利晚餐的剩菜。

column1: The remains of a Navaho ceremonial bridal pot.
column2: 納瓦霍婚禮儀式陶罐的殘骸。

column1: Do you want it?
column2: 「你要嗎？」

column1: Ello.  'Ow are you?  'Ave we met before?
column2: 「嗨。你好嗎？我們見過面嗎？」

column1: Come in, come in.  Good to see you again.  Get's kind o' tiresome sitten here an' thinken by meself all the time.
column2: 「進來進來。很高興又見到你。一個人坐在這裡想東想西有點煩人。」

column1: Let's see.  'Ow does Erasmus do it?  Oh, yes.
column2: 「讓我想想。伊拉斯謨怎麼做的？喔，對了。」

column1: Unfortunately, since the fungus on the walls shines at night so the room doesn't get dark, and the fact the hermit talks most of the night, you only get five hours rest.
column2: 很遺憾，由於牆上的真菌在夜間發光讓房間保持明亮，加上隱士幾乎整晚都在講話，你只睡了5小時。

column1: After having traded a ration, which the hermit promptly swallowed, and spending a rather boring hour of cribbage and listening to 'Enry chatter, you hit the hay.
column2: 交換了一份口糧（隱士立刻吞下肚），花了無聊的一小時玩牌戲和聽亨利嘮叨後，你就上床睡覺了。

column1: You already 'ave me only scroll.
column2: 「你已經有我唯一的捲軸了。」

column1: If you be ever needen a place to stay the night and you're in the area, I could be willen to put you up for the night for sum rations and a couple o' games o' cribbage.
column2: 「要是你以後需要地方睡覺又在這一帶，我樂意為了幾份口糧和幾盤牌戲讓你睡一晚。」

column1: Oh well, maybe sumone else can use it sumtime.
column2: 「嗯，或許別人以後用得上。」

column1: You can't sleep here, you got's no rations to give me.
column2: 「你沒法睡這裡，你沒有口糧給我。」

column1: I think you be 'avin too little magic for it to be any use to you.
column2: 「我想你的魔法太弱，對你沒用。」

column1: I'm 'Enry the 'ermit, that's me.  Me farther was an 'ermit and me murther was an 'ermit so's I come by me job rightly.
column2: 「我就是亨利隱士，沒錯。我爸爸是隱士，我媽媽也是隱士，所以我是家傳。」

column1: Don't 'ave too many visitors.  'Ermits don't, you know.  (Part o' the job description.)  I likes to see a new face, though.
column2: 「沒太多訪客。隱士就是不會有很多訪客，你知道。（那是職業的一部分。）但我還是喜歡看到新面孔。」

column1: Good to 'ear anurther's speaking besides meself.  Sos wot can I do for you?
column2: 「很高興除了自己還能聽到別人說話。那麼我能為你做什麼？」

column1: If you be wanting to learn the spell, I'll be pleased to share it to you. It's just getting molding in me 'iding place.  Do you want it?
column2: 「你要是想學法術，我樂意和你分享。它在我的躲藏地方發霉呢。」

column1: 'E summons it with a Trigger.  'Ere goes!
column2: 「他用觸發術召喚它。來吧！」

column1: There you 'as it.
column2: 「看吧，就這樣。」

column1: You can detect a faint aura of magic here.
column2: 你能感受到這裡有微弱的魔法光暈。

column1: You should wait until the hermit is asleep to check his place out.
column2: 你應該等隱士睡著才去查看他的地方。

column1: The cave is cold and clammy and it smells like mildew and wet dog hair in here!  The walls are covered with phosphorescent fungus.  The fungus glows eerily in the cave's dim light.
column2: 洞穴又冷又潮濕，聞起來像黴味和濕狗毛！牆壁上長滿了發光的真菌。真菌在洞穴的微弱光線中詭異地發光。

column1: There is no need to use magic to open anything here.
column2: 這裡沒有什麼需要魔法才能打開的。

column1: Try sleeping in the bed or on the floor -- this chair just doesn't make it.
column2: 試試睡在床上或地板上—這把椅子實在不行。

column1: The scroll vanishes even as you read the magical runes upon it.  You now have the knowledge to cast a "Trigger" spell.
column2: 當你讀著上面的魔法符文時，捲軸消失了。你現在掌握了施展「觸發術」的知識。

column1: It's on the table.
column2: 它在桌上。

column1: I don't 'ave much, but wot I 'ave, I 'ave, an' t'isn't for sale.
column2: 「我沒什麼，但我有的，我就有，不賣。」

column1: It's the headpiece of 'Enry's staff.
column2: 這是亨利魔杖的頂端。

column1: The straw is relatively fresh and looks more comfortable to lie upon than the rocks.
column2: 稻草相對新鮮，看起來比石頭舒服多了。

column1: I don't 'ave much, but wot I 'ave, I 'have, an' t'isn't for sale.
column2: 「我沒什麼，但我有的，我就有，不賣。」

column1: Yes, what you see is a table.
column2: 是的，你看到的就是桌子。

column1: The hermit has all the amenities, including running water!
column2: 隱士擁有所有設施，包括流動的水！

column1: That's funny.  It bounced right off.
column2: 真有趣。它直接彈開了。

column1: You bravely attack the bouncing beast with your bare hands.
column2: 你勇敢地用赤手空拳攻擊那隻彈跳的怪獸。

column1: The only Antwerp known in these parts has split... into parts.
column2: 這一帶唯一的安特衛普已經分裂…成碎片了。

column1: The Antwerp seems to have flown the coop.
column2: 安特衛普似乎逃脫了。

column1: By golly, it's an Antwerp!  Antwerps are on the endangered species list. They are rarely seen.
column2: 天哪，這是安特衛普！安特衛普是瀕危物種。很少見到。

column1: They look a lot like bushes.
column2: 它們看起來很像灌木。

column1: Some shrubbery, perhaps?
column2: 也許是什麼灌木叢？

column1: You're bushed.  No, wait, *they're* bushes.
column2: 你累了。不對，等等，*它們*才是灌木。

column1: The rocks were left here by a receding glacier.
column2: 這些岩石是退縮的冰河留下的。

column1: You find a keyhole concealed in a crack in the rock.
column2: 你在岩石裂縫中發現了隱藏的鑰孔。

column1: There is a narrow cave entrance among the rocks.  Inside, you see a dark passage through the hillside.
column2: 岩石之間有個狹窄的洞穴入口。裡面你看到一條穿過山坡的黑暗通道。

column1: The Antwerp seems to have flown the coop.
column2: 安特衛普似乎逃脫了。

column1: The grass is luscious, just the thing for hungry herbivores.
column2: 草很茂盛，正是饑餓的草食動物喜歡的。

column1: The way is impassable.  Sheer rock cliffs rise to serious heights.
column2: 無法通行。陡峭的岩石懸崖高聳到驚人的高度。

column1: This is an especially rocky area.
column2: 這是個特別多岩石的區域。

column1: Antwerps have too bouncy a nature to ever be truly calm.
column2: 安特衛普天性太愛彈跳，永遠無法真正平靜。

column1: Antwerps aren't dazzled easily.
column2: 安特衛普不容易被眩目。

column1: You seem to have scared the Antwerp with your behavior.
column2: 你的行為似乎嚇到了安特衛普。

column1: Cockily, you loosen up to fight.
column2: 你傲慢地放鬆身體準備打鬥。

column1: Confident and loose, you approach the Antwerp.
column2: 你自信而放鬆地走近安特衛普。

column1: Holy Mackerel!
column2: 「天哪！」

column1: Your spell has unlocked the lock on the rock, but it is not yet powerful enough to open the rock door.
column2: 你的法術打開了岩石上的鎖，但還不夠強大到打開石門。

column1: Now that the big Antwerp has split, this corner of the forest seems strangely quiet.
column2: 現在大安特衛普已經分裂，森林的這個角落變得奇異地安靜。

column1: You look around and see rocks and grass, but no Antwerp.
column2: 你環顧四周，看到岩石和草，但沒有安特衛普。

column1: You see rocks, grass and an Antwerp.
column2: 你看到岩石、草和一隻安特衛普。

column1: The forest extends to the east and north.
column2: 森林向東和向北延伸。

column1: It's a rock, not a charcoal briquette!
column2: 這是塊岩石，不是木炭！

column1: Despite your mightiest efforts, the rock does not move.
column2: 儘管你盡力了，岩石還是沒有動。

column1: The rock door has already been opened.
column2: 石門已經打開了。

column1: You are not strong enough yet to open the rock door.
column2: 你還不夠強大無法打開石門。

column1: The key glows for an instant as you insert it into the lock, but nothing else happens.
column2: 當你把鑰匙插入鎖孔時，鑰匙閃了一下，但沒有其他事發生。

column1: The lock in the rock clicks open.
column2: 岩石上的鎖發出喀噠一聲打開了。

column1: The lock is beyond your present skill.  It might help if you had the key.
column2: 這把鎖超出你現在的能力。有鑰匙的話可能會有幫助。

column1: You'd have a much easier time of this if you had the key.
column2: 有鑰匙的話會容易得多。

column1: Ah, got it!  The lock in the rock clicks open.
column2: 啊，成功了！岩石上的鎖發出喀噠一聲打開了。

column1: ... Or at least a better set of tools.
column2: …或至少一套更好的工具。
